title: LACA
plain_summary: The LACA bill makes it easier for people in two cities to go to federal court. It says that federal courts can now hold court sessions in College Station, Texas and El Centro, California. Before this law, people in these areas had to travel farther to attend federal court. Now they can go to court in their own cities instead of having to go somewhere else.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Local Access to Courts Act or LACA.

sections.title: Section 2 Organization of Texas District Courts
sections.text: This section changes the law that describes where federal courts in Texas can hold their sessions. It adds College Station as a place where one of the Texas federal district courts can meet.

sections.title: Section 3 Organization of California District Courts
sections.text: This section changes the law that describes where federal courts in California can hold their sessions. It adds El Centro as a place where the federal district court can meet, in addition to San Diego.
